methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Fleet Space Technologies is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-market equivalents, with particular focus on 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($70M) as a proxy and applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $119M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $101M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $810M. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between stage-progression (which anchors to capital raised) and peer positioning (which reflects market consensus for operational and technical maturity in this cohort). Key uncertainties include Fleet's revenue trajectory relative to peer benchmarks and whether the company's technical milestones and customer traction justify peer-level valuation multiples.
